notes: Telang 2011 Phase II — DD monotherapy in melanoma. Abstract extraction.
title: Phase II trial of the regulatory T cell-depleting agent, denileukin diftitox, in patients with unresectable stage IV melanoma
excluded_reason: Full-text backfill 2026-04-23 revealed Telang 2011 BMC Cancer Phase II DD trial did NOT measure Tregs in study patients — Methods describe only imaging, adverse events, and clinical endpoints. The paper references Treg depletion effects from prior work (Rasku 2008) but made no direct flow cytometry or FoxP3 measurements on this cohort. Fails screening criterion 3 (at least one Treg PD readout in an in-scope tissue). Title is misleading; actual Treg-depletion demonstration is in the referenced earlier Rasku publication.

intervention: PORTER platform: multi-arm including ICI combinations
notes: Galsky 2025 PORTER — multi-cohort platform; Treg PD a correlative objective. Full-text needed for arm-specific numeric values.
title: Clinical and Translational Results from PORTER, a Multicohort Phase I Platform Trial of Combination Immunotherapy in Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer
excluded_reason: Full-text backfill 2026-04-23 revealed PORTER is a 3-arm nivolumab combination platform in mCRPC (nivo+cytokine, nivo+RT+DC activation, nivo+cancer vaccine). None of the three arms targets Treg depletion mechanistically; Treg was not a pre-specified endpoint. The paper's only Treg mention describes baseline Tregs (among CD39+ CD4+ cells) as a correlate of BETTER disease control — the opposite direction from depletion. Fails screening criteria 2 (no Treg-targeting intervention) and 6 (intent-to-deplete gate: no explicit Treg-modulation rationale; exploratory correlative at best, and direction is wrong). Reclassifying as incidental-observation from a non-Treg-targeted platform trial.
